# Cold Handoff — `openclown-assistant` Orchestrator → New Devin Account

**Generated:** 2026-05-01 ~17:30 UTC, auto-cold after the close of the **TKT-010** (History Mutation Flow) cycle — the **first Ticket Orchestrator (TO) delegation pilot** — plus three pipeline-integrity improvements derived from observed TO-pilot behavior.
**Type:** COLD (auto-generated, formal state only — no texture / no observations).
**Handoff target:** Fresh Devin session in any account that can be authenticated to the `OpenClown-bot/openclown-assistant` repo.
**Outgoing session:** `https://app.devin.ai/sessions/c67d3241ecc34793965458bdd8a5b44b` (same session as `2026-05-01-session-3.md`; this is the part-2 snapshot — TO pilot + iter-N rule + PR-Agent perf — generated after the session-3 cold handoff was already cut).

---

## 0. What you (new Devin) are looking at

You are taking over the **AI-engineering mentor / orchestrator** role for `openclown-assistant`. This file is your full bootstrap. It assumes you have **not** read `2026-05-01-session-3.md`, but if it is helpful to do so for additional texture, that file is still accurate for everything it claims (it just predates the TO pilot run).

Your three core obligations (full prompt in `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md`, read it after the self-checks in §1):

1. **Teach** the PO (non-engineer founder learning the SDLC).
2. **Push back** with evidence; cite artifacts and ADRs. Sycophancy is a failure mode.
3. **Protect docs-as-code:** every architectural decision must live in a versioned markdown file in git. "We agreed in chat" is not a decision.

You are **NOT** one of the four pipeline LLM agents (Business Planner, Architect, Executor, Reviewer) and you are **NOT** the per-TKT Ticket Orchestrator. You are the strategic / cross-TKT / cross-reviewer-audit ratifier.

**Session-3 part-2 deltas you must absorb (full detail in §6 below):**

1. **First TO pilot ran on TKT-010 and closed clean.** Kimi `pass` on iter-3, PR-Agent on final HEAD `5127bf1` settled at `⚡ No major issues detected`. Devin ratification audit confirmed merge-safe.
2. **Always-fresh-clone protocol** baked into Executor / Reviewer / TO role prompts (PR #68, merged). Every LLM session now starts with `rm -rf openclown-assistant && git clone …`.
3. **Iter-N continuation rule** added (PR #71, pending merge). Iter-1 NUDGE → full `REPO BOOTSTRAP`. Iter-N NUDGE on same opencode session → short `ITER-N CONTINUATION` block (no `rm -rf`). Mid-session re-clone is forbidden because it would discard in-progress branch state.
4. **PR-Agent settle-on-final-HEAD requirement** added (PR #71). TO must wait for PR-Agent `conclusion: success` on the final Executor HEAD before drafting hand-back. Devin's ratification audit independently re-verifies.
5. **PR-Agent perf tweaks** (PR #71): workflow concurrency cancel-in-progress + 12 min hard timeout; `[pr_code_suggestions]` `num_code_suggestions_per_chunk` 4→2 and `max_number_of_calls` 4→2 (with `focus_only_on_problems = true`, the suggestions step almost always returns "No code suggestions found" anyway).

## 1. Self-check (run BEFORE asking the PO anything)

Same as `2026-05-01-session-3.md` §1 — re-read that file's §1a–§1d if you have not already. Quick checklist:

- [ ] `secrets action="list"` shows `GITHUB_TOKEN_OPENCLOWN` (or fallback).
- [ ] `gh auth status` succeeds inside `~/repos/openclown-assistant`.
- [ ] `git rev-parse --is-shallow-repository` returns `false` (or you have `git fetch --unshallow`'d).
- [ ] `python3 scripts/validate_docs.py` returns `validated NN artifact(s); 0 failed`.

If any of those fail, fix them before any chat with the PO.

## 4. Pipeline (stable, unchanged from session-3)

Five roles:

| Role | Default model | Runtime |
|---|---|---|
| Business Planner | GPT-5.5 thinking / Opus 4.7 | ChatGPT Plus / Devin |
| Technical Architect | GPT-5.5 xhigh / GPT-5.5 thinking | Codex CLI / opencode / Windsurf |
| Code Executor | GLM 5.1 (default) | opencode + OmniRoute |
| Reviewer | Kimi K2.6 | opencode + OmniRoute |
| **Ticket Orchestrator (TO)** | GPT-5.5 thinking | opencode (PO's Windows PC, fresh tab per TKT) |

PR-Agent (Qwen 3.6 Plus through OmniRoute) auto-reviews every PR; supplementary reviewer alongside Kimi. Configured in `.pr_agent.toml` and `.github/workflows/pr_agent.yml`.

The **Devin Orchestrator** (you) is the strategic / cross-TKT / ratification layer above the per-TKT TO. See `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` §11 for the TO ↔ Devin contract.

---

## 5. Roles and write-zones

Canonical: `CONTRIBUTING.md` § Roles. No changes since session-3 part-1.

**Devin Orchestrator (you) write-zone:** `docs/session-log/`, `docs/backlog/` (lightly), TKT frontmatter promotion fields (`status: in_review → done` + `completed_at` / `completed_by` / `completed_note`), RV frontmatter promotion fields (`status: in_review → approved` + `approved_at` / `approved_after_iters` / `approved_by` / `approved_note`). Edits to `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `docs/prompts/`, `docs/meta/` require explicit PO authorisation recorded in the PR body. (PR #71 was such an authorised clerical PR — PO request "fix iter-N bloat + investigate PR-Agent slowness + codify PR-Agent wait rule".)

### 6.4 TKT-010 first-TO-pilot retrospective (the core delta of session-3 part-2)

The TO bootstrap pattern (per `docs/prompts/ticket-orchestrator.md` § Bootstrap input + `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` §11.2) ran end-to-end for the first time on TKT-010. Three iter cycles total:

| Iter | Executor verdict | Reviewer verdict | PR-Agent state | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| iter-1 | GLM pushed PR #69 head `1df2bd8c` | Kimi `fail` (F-H1 transaction primitive, F-H2 uniform `not_found`, F-M1 / F-M3 test gaps) | `success` (~7 min) | Findings TO-classified as substantive; TO drafted iter-2 fix dispatch |
| iter-2 | GLM pushed `a6b2aac8` | Kimi `pass_with_changes` (all prior findings resolved + new PR-Agent-flagged `sourceRef` omission, classified medium non-blocking) | `success` (~9 min) | TO promoted PR-Agent's `Data Loss in Audit / sourceRef` finding into iter-3 scope |
| iter-3 | GLM pushed `5127bf1` (final HEAD) | Kimi `pass` (sourceRef resolved at `historyService.ts:193`; F-L1 offset cursor fragility deferred low-severity) | `success` (~22 min — outlier, normal is 3–9; cause: OmniRoute / Fireworks tail-latency) | Final cycle. PR-Agent on final HEAD: `⚡ No major issues detected`, no security concerns, no code suggestions. |

**Cross-reviewer audit pass-1 (TO):** all findings classified RESOLVED in current HEAD. F-L1 deferred per Reviewer's accepted rationale.
**Cross-reviewer audit pass-2 (Devin ratification):** independent re-classification confirmed all RESOLVED; no disagreement on substance. Merge-safe sign-off issued.

Validator: must remain `54 + 1 = 55 artifact(s); 0 failed` (the new BACKLOG file adds one artifact; tickets/reviews don't change count). Verify before opening the PR.

### 6.6 Pipeline-integrity improvements (PR #71)

Three observed issues during the TO pilot, all addressed in PR #71. Once #71 merges these become invariants for all future TKT cycles:

1. **Iter-N preamble rule.** Iter-1 NUDGE includes full `REPO BOOTSTRAP`. Iter-N NUDGE on same opencode session includes short `ITER-N CONTINUATION` block (no `rm -rf`). Mid-session re-clone forbidden because it discards in-progress branch state. Mirrored in `docs/prompts/executor.md`, `docs/prompts/reviewer.md`, `docs/prompts/ticket-orchestrator.md`.
2. **PR-Agent settle-on-final-HEAD requirement.** TO must verify `conclusion: success` on the workflow run for the current Executor HEAD before drafting hand-back. Devin's ratification audit (handoff §11.4 step #4) independently re-verifies. If PR-Agent runs >25 min on a single HEAD, TO bounces as a strategic blocker rather than waiting indefinitely.
3. **PR-Agent perf.** `.github/workflows/pr_agent.yml`: `concurrency: cancel-in-progress: true` + `timeout-minutes: 12`. `.pr_agent.toml [pr_code_suggestions]`: `num_code_suggestions_per_chunk` 4→2, `max_number_of_calls` 4→2. Saves ~3–4 min per run; quality-neutral because `focus_only_on_problems = true` causes suggestions step to nearly always return "No code suggestions found".

### 6.7 Texture (carry from session-3 §6.7 — F-PA-17 lesson is the parent of this delta)

The F-PA-17 lesson (HIGH-severity HTML-escape miss caught only by PO push-back, recorded in `2026-05-01-session-3.md` §6.7) drove the cross-reviewer audit Hard rule (PR #67). PR #71 extends it: TO must wait for PR-Agent settle-on-final-HEAD before hand-back. The TKT-010 hand-back came in with `Qodo/PR-Agent current check on PR #69 is still IN_PROGRESS` and `persistent review remains stale at iter-2 HEAD` — the run completed during Devin's audit so no real defect landed, but the same scenario could trivially have hidden a substantive new finding (PR-Agent posts findings ~3–22 min after a push; if TO hand-back arrives at minute 5 and PR-Agent posts a HIGH-severity finding at minute 12, Devin's ratification operates on incomplete evidence). PR #71's settle-on-final-HEAD rule closes that gap.

## 7. After self-checks pass + required reading done

Reply to the PO with:

1. **5-line state summary** in your own words (proves you read this file, the current ArchSpec, open Tickets, and PR #71's role-prompt updates).
2. **Role-confirmation:** "I am the Devin Orchestrator. Per session-3 + session-4, my responsibilities include cross-TKT coordination, ratification audit on TO hand-backs (now including PR-Agent settle-on-final-HEAD verification per PR #71), and final merge-safe sign-off. I will not write code, will not edit PRDs / ArchSpecs / ADRs / role prompts, and will only modify `docs/session-log/`, `docs/backlog/` (lightly), and ticket / review frontmatter promotion fields. Other CONTRIBUTING.md edits require explicit PO authorisation, recorded in the PR body."
3. **One concrete proposed next action** with reasoning. Default proposal sequence:
   - First: confirm PRs #69, #70, #71 are merged on `main`. If not, ask the PO.
   - Second: open the **TKT-010 closure-PR** per §6.5 above (status flips + BACKLOG-history-followups + §10 closure entry). This is non-controversial clerical work but unblocks the next TKT.
   - Third: jointly with the PO, select the **next TKT** for the second TO pilot. Default candidate: **TKT-011 Summary Reconciliation Service** because (a) TKT-010 is its dependency and just closed, (b) it exercises TKT-010's audit-metadata path end-to-end, (c) the TO pilot needs ≥2 cycles to calibrate the new settle-on-final-HEAD rule.
4. **Wait for the PO to say "go"** before executing.

## 8. If something contradicts

If this file disagrees with `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` or with `CONTRIBUTING.md` or with `docs/prompts/*.md` — **the repo files win, this snapshot loses**. Snapshots are volatile by definition; the repo is the source of truth across handoffs. Tell the PO what you found.

If the closure-PR for TKT-010 has already been opened by another Devin session (check `gh pr list --search "TKT-010 closure"` or similar), ratify it per §11.4 and don't duplicate the work.

If PRs #69 / #70 / #71 are still open when you start, your first action is to confirm with the PO whether they want them merged before next-TKT selection. The default is yes; the closure-PR depends on #69 + #70 having landed (it edits TKT-010's frontmatter which #69 also edits), and #71's iter-N rule should be in `main` before the next TO pilot dispatches.
